segments.title: Hood sister ships as carrier conversions
segments.question: When did Dr. Clarke discuss turning HMS Hood's sister ships into aircraft carriers?
segments.answerShort: He points viewers to the battlecruiser, dreadnought, and treaty episodes, then explains that converting the cancelled Admiral-class sisters would have produced carriers larger than Courageous, Glorious, and Furious.
segments.summary: A Bruships Q&A revisits the idea of converting HMS Hood's cancelled sister ships into large Royal Navy aircraft carriers.
segments.body: In this carrier-conversion exchange, Dr. Clarke points viewers back to battlecruiser, dreadnought, and treaty discussions where the idea has appeared before. He treats the cancelled Admiral-class sister ships as plausible large carrier-conversion candidates, larger than Courageous, Glorious, and Furious, and describes them as potential vice-admiral-sized fleet carriers rather than small stopgap conversions.

evidence.note: The prompt asks where the earlier Hood sister-ship carrier-conversion discussion appeared, and the answer points to battlecruiser, dreadnought, and treaty episodes.
The answer explains that the idea was considered, then compares the possible conversions with Courageous, Glorious, and Furious and calls them potential vice-admiral aircraft carriers.
